events.name: 10th MIT Global Humanities Forum on “Rethinking healing—from ancient wisdom to modern medicine” on May 8 (online)
events.description: Dear colleagues, students, and friends, Join us for the 2026 GHI 10th Forum! This session introduces the GHI Pillar Medicine & the Healing Arts, and its projects. 10th GHI Forum Title: Rethinking healing—from ancient wisdom to modern medicineDate: May 8, 2026, 10:00–11:30 AM ESTWhere: Online (Zoom Registration Link: LINK)Speakers: Ronit Yoeli-Tlalim, Michael Stanley-Baker, Joaquín Terrones 【Abstract】 What can history teach us about the big challenges faced by medicine in the world today? Our pillar is tapping into historically rooted medical knowledge as resources for new directions for enhancing both medical practice and public understanding of health and well-being. We examine…

events.name: Nexus: Connecting, Learning, Advancing - A Staff Conference
events.description: Staff conference designed to promote community, cross-training, learning, and excellence across Northeastern University staff members. This year’s conference will focus on three core themes: Connecting: Creating community for staff across campuses, colleges, and departments and breaking down silos. Learning: Sharing knowledge to encourage professional development and best practices across the university. Advancing: Practical strategies on advancing within our careers as university administrators and staff

events.name: Fulbright Spain Webinar
events.description: Interested in doing research in Spain through the Fulbright U.S. Student Program? Join us for a webinar with members of the Fulbright Spain Commission and an MIT alumna currently on a Fulbright in the country. You’ll learn about Spanish research centers and universities with specialties across many fields and gain insight into what Spain is looking for in its Fulbright applicants. The Fulbright US Student Program is open to students and recent alumni who are US citizens and will not have a PhD by October 6, 2026. We can also discuss the Fulbright Spain Postdoctoral Award and Fulbright Scholar research…

events.name: Fulbright: Preparing for the MIT Internal Deadline
events.description: Interested in applying to Fulbright? Learn what you need to do this summer to prepare for MIT’s internal deadline in August. MIT’s Fulbright Program Advisor, Julia Mongo, will explain the process and the requirements and answer your questions. (If you did not previously attend one of our Fulbright Information Sessions, email jmongo@mit.edu, and Julia will send you a copy of the video and slides.) Fulbright U.S. Student Program applicants must be US citizens who will have completed at least a BS degree by the grant start date in fall 2027 / spring 2028. This event is open to undergraduates (current…

events.name: Pray It Forward
events.description: continues! The C21 Center, in partnership with the BC Alumni Association, would like to invite you to spend just 15 minutes during the week to reflect and recharge. All are welcome! Join us on Wednesdays from 4:00–4:15pm ET on Zoom for a brief time-out to pray: reading the day's Gospel, listening to a brief reflection, offering prayer intentions, and sharing a group prayer relevant to the week. Register here to receive the Zoom link and reminders!

events.name: The Lumpen and the Miscreant: Mary Walling Blackburn presents Cream Psychosis, in conversation with David Levine
events.location: Carpenter Center for the Visual Arts, Theater, Lower Level 24 Quincy St, Cambridge, MA 02138
events.description: “When an empire is lurching to a halt at its very end, it might be the moment when it begins, or is forced, to re-imagine its relationship to a national insanity.” —Mary Walling Blackburn “XOXO, Insanity, Institution” (2011) The Carpenter Center for the Visual Arts and MIT List Visual Arts Center co-present a reading of Cream Psychosis (Sternberg Press, e-flux Journal, 2026), a collection of essays spanning the 2010s to the present by artist and writer Mary Walling Blackburn, followed by a conversation between Walling Blackburn and artist and writer David Levine. Moving with near psychedelic precision across American time…

events.name: Wargaming 4 All
events.description: Description: Build and train AI agents to compete in an e-sports–style video game that simulates a realistic and complex military operation. Participants use a game simulation and a reinforcement-learning environment to develop their own agents and compete head-to-head. The top teams are eligible for a cash prize and their agents will face Air Force pilots in a final exhibition. Registration on the website is mandatory. Website: https://war.game Areas of tangential interest: reinforcement learning and AI training, multi-agent systems, human machine teaming, modeling and simulation Course timeline: Course timeline: Now: teams form, register on the website, build and train their AI…

events.name: Jesuit Studies Café
events.description: The Institute for Advanced Jesuit Studies and its collaborating partners invite you to join informal conversations with the world's preeminent scholars working on the history, spirituality, and educational heritage of the Society of Jesus. This semester's discussions center around featured scholars and their projects or published works. These discussions – hosted by the Institute over coffee and available via Zoom videoconference on the indicated Thursdays monthly – are unique opportunities to learn more about the newest and most interesting scholarship in Jesuit Studies. If you have any questions, please contact the Institute (iajs@bc.edu). If you would like to attend a…

events.name: Tour: Easy Company: England to the Eagle's Nest
events.description: Join fellow BC alumni and friends on an unforgettable journey to visit major European sites of World War II, as experienced by Easy Company. The best-selling book, Band of Brothers, was adapted into the HBO miniseries that brought the story of Easy Company to millions of viewers worldwide. Perhaps no single work has had such an impact or made such an imprint on the American memory of World War II. This extraordinary tour across seven countries in Europe highlights the beaches, bridges, cities, and villages where the legendary Easy Company fought the crucial D-Day battles and changed the course of…

events.name: AI in Education: A Conversation for Beginners
events.description: Artificial Intelligence is becoming part of everyday academic and professional life, but many people are still unsure how it works or how it should be used. This discussion-based workshop invites participants who are curious about AI to explore what it is, how it is being used, and what it might mean for learning and future careers. Through open conversation and shared perspectives, participants will reflect on the opportunities and challenges AI brings to education and work. Register here!

events.name: Tour: A Voyage from Venice to Athens
events.description: Cruise the Adriatic with fellow BC Beyond travelers on a voyage from Venice to Athens aboard the luxurious cruise ship, EXPLORA II. Beginning in Venice, tour the highlights and gems of this fascinating floating city. After embarking the ship, visit several lovely and historic towns in Croatia—Rovinj, Porec, and Zadar—culminating in a tour of Dubrovnik with its majestic medieval walls. Further south, venture into Italy’s Puglia region, and after a day at sea, stop at Greece’s sun-kissed island of Milos. The voyage concludes in Athens, with the opportunity to extend the tour to visit the major sites of this bustling…
